title: prunus divaricata subsp. borealicaucasica
intro: Prunus divaricata subsp. borealicaucasica, also known as the Caucasian cherry plum, is a subspecies of the Prunus divaricata tree native to the Caucasus region. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree with edible fruits and leaves that have been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The leaves and bark of Prunus divaricata subsp. borealicaucasica have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate coughs, and soothe digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems, skin conditions, and digestive issues.
